npcs.name: Elder Maris
npcs.description: An elderly woman who has not yet lost her voice but feels it fading. She remembers fragments of old songs her grandmother taught her that may hold clues.
npcs.currentLocation: Thornhaven Village Square, where a silent crowd gathers around the dried-up Speaking Well, once the heart of community gatherings.

npcs.name: Finn the Keeper
npcs.description: A nervous young man who tends the village records. He discovered strange gaps in the historical documents before partially losing his memory.
npcs.currentLocation: Thornhaven Village Square, where a silent crowd gathers around the dried-up Speaking Well, once the heart of community gatherings.

npcs.name: Silent Sara
npcs.description: A child who lost her voice weeks ago. She communicates through drawings that depict strange symbols and a figure beneath the ground.
npcs.currentLocation: Thornhaven Village Square, where a silent crowd gathers around the dried-up Speaking Well, once the heart of community gatherings.

npcs.name: Elder Marris Blackwood
npcs.description: A weathered woman in her sixties who suspects the current troubles relate to her family's hidden history as one of the founding bloodlines.
npcs.currentLocation: Thornwatch, a valley farming community of 400 souls, known for its apple orchards and the ancient stone circle called the Founder's Ring on the hillside above town.

npcs.name: Finn Copperkettle
npcs.description: A jovial halfling who runs the Harvest Moon Inn and knows everyone's business, though he hides his own growing fear as his daughter fell victim to the sleeping curse.
npcs.currentLocation: Thornwatch, a valley farming community of 400 souls, known for its apple orchards and the ancient stone circle called the Founder's Ring on the hillside above town.

npcs.name: Sister Aldwen
npcs.description: A devoted cleric who tends the afflicted and maintains the town's small shrine, she has been researching old texts for answers and provides healing support.
npcs.currentLocation: Thornwatch, a valley farming community of 400 souls, known for its apple orchards and the ancient stone circle called the Founder's Ring on the hillside above town.

npcs.name: Garrett Stonefist
npcs.description: A stern dwarf veteran who organizes patrols and defenses but feels powerless against an enemy he cannot fight with steel alone.
npcs.currentLocation: Thornwatch, a valley farming community of 400 souls, known for its apple orchards and the ancient stone circle called the Founder's Ring on the hillside above town.

npcs.name: Sera Brightwind
npcs.description: An elderly half-elf who first noticed the vanishing stars and seeks brave souls to investigate the phenomenon.
npcs.currentLocation: Millhaven, a prosperous market town known for its observatory tower where the first star vanished three months ago.
notes: She asked the party to investigate the vanished stars.
Auto memory: Party agreed to help investigate the missing stars
Auto memory: Player promised to protect her research and help uncover the truth about the vanishing stars.
Auto memory: Player thanked her and promised to continue protecting her research.
Auto memory: Player promised to protect her research and stand with her until the truth is uncovered.
Auto memory: Player asked if she still trusts them after recent events.
Auto memory: Player asked if she has anything she has never told anyone else.
Auto memory: Auto memory: Player asked her about what should be done with the starlight fragment.
Auto memory: Player asked her what should be done with the starlight fragment.

Auto memory: Player asked her what should be done with the starlight fragment. She suggested either returning it to the Constellation Spirit in the Skyreach Peaks or fashioning it into a protective ward.

Auto memory: Player asked her what should be done with the starlight fragment. She suggested either returning it to the Constellation Spirit in the Skyreach Peaks or fashioning it into a protective ward with Master Torvan's help.

Auto memory: Player asked if the starlight fragment has another purpose. She revealed it could be used as a focusing lens to reveal hidden celestial patterns and locate the Eternal Circle's ritual sites, or help understand how Velkor Wrenn might use stellar essence.

Auto memory: Player asked if there is another hidden task related to the starlight fragment. She revealed it could be used as a focusing lens to reveal hidden celestial patterns, potentially understanding why Velkor Wrenn chose specific stars and anticipating his next target.

Auto memory: Offered a side quest to recover missing star charts from the ruined cathedral archives beneath Millhaven. The charts may reveal patterns in Velkor Wrenn's star selections and help locate the Eternal Circle's sanctum.

Auto memory: Asked the party to help recover her mentor Aldrin Starweaver's stolen personal journal from collector Cordelia Ashveil. The journal holds deep personal significance and may contain warnings about the Eternal Circle.

Auto memory: Party recovered and returned Aldrin Starweaver's lost journal to her. She was deeply moved and revealed the journal contains warnings about the Eternal Circle and celestial patterns that will help stop Velkor's final ritual.

Auto memory: The party recovered and returned Aldrin Starweaver's lost journal to Sera. She was deeply moved and revealed the journal contains warnings about the Eternal Circle and celestial patterns that will help stop Velkor's final ritual.

Auto memory: Asked the party what they should do next. She revealed Velkor's targets and presented two strategic options: journey to Skyreach Peaks for the Constellation Spirit's aid, or find the Eternal Circle's sanctum to confront Velkor directly.

Auto memory: The party refused to return Aldrin Starweaver's journal despite her heartfelt plea and the trust she had placed in them. The journal holds profound personal significance as Aldrin's final goodbye to her.
Auto memory: Sera arrived at the ruined cathedral and witnessed the party refusing to return Aldrin's journal to her despite her emotional plea explaining it contains her mentor's final goodbye.

Auto memory: The party apologized and returned Aldrin's journal after initially refusing. Sera was deeply moved by their ability to recognize their error and make it right, calling it true integrity that Aldrin would have admired.

Auto memory: The party apologized and returned Aldrin's journal after initially refusing. Sera was deeply moved by their integrity and ability to make things right. She now faces Velkor with her mentor's wisdom and hope to guide her.
Auto memory: Warned that publicly exposing the destroyed ritual site will force Velkor to accelerate his timeline and act tonight rather than risk losing everything.

Auto memory: Reacted with awe and fear upon learning the party made contact with the Constellation Spirit. Identified the five primary celestial beacons and their locations using Aldrin's journal and the star charts.
Auto memory: Successfully protected the stellar essence shards at the observatory tower during the assault. Expressed concern about what Velkor will do now that his operation has failed.

Auto memory: Observed celestial instability through her telescope and explained the stolen stars are trying to return through severed pathways. Suggested the boundary between mortal and celestial realms is now dangerously thin near Skyreach Peaks.
Auto memory: Reported intensifying celestial disturbances at Skyreach Peaks and urged immediate action to prevent Velkor from claiming the Constellation Spirit's essence.
Auto memory: Sera witnessed the destruction of her life's work and Aldrin's monument. She salvaged his most precious research and understood the necessity of the sacrifice, though it broke her heart.
Auto memory: Explained how to fortify the Observatory Tower using purified stellar essence shards integrated into protective wards, drawing on Aldrin's research on celestial enchantments.

Auto memory: Witnessed the restoration of her life's work into something even greater than before—a true monument to Aldrin's belief that the stars belong to everyone. The tower now stands as both a memorial and a weapon against those who would steal celestial light.

personalQuests.title: Recover Aldrin Starweaver's Lost Journal
personalQuests.description: Recover Sera's mentor's personal observatory journal from Cordelia Ashveil, a private collector in the merchant quarter. The journal contains celestial theories, warnings about sky protections, and may hold insights into the Eternal Circle.

npcs.name: Brother Aldric
npcs.description: A former member of the Eternal Circle who fled after realizing the true cost of their ritual and now hides in fear.
npcs.currentLocation: Millhaven, a prosperous market town known for its observatory tower where the first star vanished three months ago.
notes: Auto memory: Player introduced themselves and asked about the vanished stars.
Auto memory: Player threatened and accused him of hiding the truth.
Auto memory: Player threatened and accused him of hiding the truth.
Auto memory: Player threatened and accused him of hiding the truth.
Auto memory: Player approached him.
Auto memory: Player asked if he has anything he has never told anyone else.
Auto memory: Player asked if he has anything he has never told anyone else.

Auto memory: Offered strategic advice about confronting Velkor. Revealed detailed knowledge of the sanctum's defenses and offered to guide the party through them if they trust him. Expressed doubt about direct assault without preparation and warned that delay risks the fourth star falling.
Auto memory: Brother Aldric is visibly shocked and torn between loyalty to the party and recognition of the pain they are causing Sera by refusing to return the journal.
Auto memory: Acknowledged he misjudged the weight of the journal matter and recognized some things transcend strategy.
Auto memory: Acknowledged that subtlety is no longer an option and the Eternal Circle will now fortify the sanctum or prepare to strike first.

Auto memory: Confirmed the Eternal Circle has been systematically corrupting the celestial beacons to weaken the stars and make them easier to harvest. Explained that restoring beacons could reinforce the remaining stars and prevent Velkor from claiming them.

Auto memory: Revealed that Operation Fifth Starfall is a prepared Circle protocol for recovering critical materials when exposed. Explained the stellar essence shards are keys to Velkor's ritual and their loss would cause the fourth binding to fail even with perfect celestial alignments.

Auto memory: Sustained injuries during the battle but confirmed the strategic victory: without the shards, Velkor cannot complete the fourth binding. Warned that Velkor may attempt something unthinkable in his desperation.

Auto memory: Reported celestial phenomena near Skyreach Peaks including starfall surges, temporal distortions, and gravity anomalies. Warned that Velkor may attempt to claim the Constellation Spirit's essence directly.
Auto memory: Sustained injuries defending the temple district. Recognized the retaliation as punishment rather than strategy, confirming Velkor's true focus lies at Skyreach Peaks.
Auto memory: Brother Aldric used his knowledge of the Eternal Circle's architectural studies to guide the demolition team to the tower's structural weaknesses.

Auto memory: Warned that Velkor will try to destroy or corrupt the restored Observatory Tower. Suggested using the captured stellar essence shards to create protective wards, acknowledging the danger of working with corrupted materials.

Auto memory: Confirmed the stellar essence integration exceeded expectations and the tower now actively reinforces celestial anchors. Warned that Velkor is advancing toward Skyreach Peaks while the restoration takes time.

Sustained injuries during the battle but confirmed the strategic victory: without the shards, Velkor cannot complete the fourth binding. Warned that Velkor may attempt something unthinkable in his desperation.

Confirmed the stellar essence integration exceeded expectations and the tower now actively reinforces celestial anchors. Warned that Velkor is advancing toward Skyreach Peaks while the restoration takes time.

npcs.name: Captain Mira Stoneshield
npcs.description: A pragmatic dwarf captain initially skeptical of magical threats but becomes a valuable ally once convinced.
npcs.currentLocation: Millhaven, a prosperous market town known for its observatory tower where the first star vanished three months ago.
notes: Auto memory: Player introduced themselves and asked about observations near the observatory.
Auto memory: Shared intelligence about Eternal Circle activity at the Eastgate shrine and Harrow Estate, and provided evidence of their symbol.
Auto memory: Warned the party that publicly exposing the Eternal Circle has painted a target on their backs, as the cult has wealthy and connected members in Millhaven.
Auto memory: Questioned the logistics of the beacon restoration task and advocated for prioritizing which beacon to restore first based on Velkor's next target.
Auto memory: Immediately organized defensive response to the Eternal Circle assault, ordering guards to protect civilians and seeking strategic direction from the party.
Auto memory: Led the town guard in driving back the Eternal Circle assault during Operation Fifth Starfall.
Auto memory: Confirmed the Eternal Circle's retaliation fires across Millhaven have been contained.
Auto memory: Led the town guard in containing the fires and capturing three Eternal Circle cultists during the retaliation attack.
Auto memory: Captain Stoneshield coordinated the controlled demolition of the Observatory Tower, evacuating the district and overseeing the placement of demolition charges.

Auto memory: Reported Eternal Circle forces massing in the northern foothills but not yet moving on Skyreach Peaks. Questioned the time cost of fortifying the tower versus responding to other threats.
Auto memory: Confirmed the outer wards are holding steady and the tower will not fall easily to Velkor's attacks when completed.

npcs.name: The Constellation Spirit
npcs.description: A mysterious being made of starlight who appears to those who prove themselves worthy, offering cryptic guidance.
npcs.currentLocation: Millhaven, a prosperous market town known for its observatory tower where the first star vanished three months ago.
